recentAddition: Chinchilla: Training Compute-Optimal Large Language Models (Hoffmann, Borgeaud, Mensch et al., 2022, NeurIPS) - DeepMind's landmark paper that redefined LLM scaling laws. Showed that 70B parameters with 1.4T tokens outperform larger undertrained models (Gopher 280B, GPT-3 175B, Megatron 530B). Key finding: model size and training data should scale equally for compute-optimal training (~20:1 token-to-parameter ratio). Chinchilla achieved 67.5% on MMLU (+7.5% over Gopher) with 4× fewer parameters. arXiv:2203.15556.
